name: Taiwan Presidential Election - Lai Ching-te Wins
description: Lai Ching-te of the Democratic Progressive Party won Taiwan's presidential election with 40% of the vote, defying Beijing's preferred outcome.

name: China Water Cannon Attack on Philippine Resupply Vessel
description: Two China Coast Guard ships simultaneously fired water cannons at the Philippine Navy-chartered vessel during a routine resupply mission to Second Thomas Shoal, shattering the windshield and injuring four crew members.

name: Second Water Cannon Attack at Second Thomas Shoal
casualties: 4 Philippine Navy personnel injured; vessel heavily damaged
description: China Coast Guard vessels blasted the repaired Unaizah May 4 with water cannons for nearly an hour during another resupply attempt.

name: US Typhon Mid-Range Missile System Deployed to Philippines
description: The US Army deployed the Typhon missile launcher, capable of firing Tomahawk cruise missiles and SM-6 at ranges of 500-2,000 km, to northern Luzon for the first time.

name: Balikatan 2024 - Largest US-Philippine Joint Exercise
description: The 39th iteration of Balikatan included live-fire exercises in the South China Sea and the first-ever joint sinking exercise targeting a decommissioned ship.

name: USS Halsey FONOP Near Paracel Islands
target: China's excessive maritime claims around Paracel Islands
description: USS Halsey conducted the first publicly announced FONOP of 2024, asserting innocent passage near the Paracel Islands to challenge China's straight baselines declaration.

name: Lai Ching-te Inaugurated as Taiwan President
description: Lai Ching-te was inaugurated as Taiwan's president. His speech referenced Taiwan as 'a sovereign and independent state,' which Beijing labeled a 'confession of Taiwan independence.'

name: PLA Joint Sword-2024A Exercises Around Taiwan
target: Taiwan and outlying islands (Kinmen, Matsu, Wuqiu, Dongyin)
description: Two days after Lai's inauguration, the PLA launched Joint Sword-2024A, surrounding Taiwan with naval, air, and rocket force drills in six zones simulating precision strikes and joint operations.

name: Aircraft Carrier Fujian Begins Sea Trials
description: China's third and most advanced aircraft carrier, the Fujian (Type 003), began sea trials from Shanghai, becoming the first Chinese carrier equipped with electromagnetic aircraft launch systems.

name: China Coast Guard Boards Philippine Navy Boat at Second Thomas Shoal
casualties: 8 Filipino personnel injured; 1 lost a finger
description: Chinese forces surrounded, rammed, and boarded a Philippine Navy inflatable boat during a Sierra Madre resupply mission, wielding knives and axes to destroy equipment.

name: China-Philippines Confrontation at Sabina Shoal
target: Philippine Coast Guard vessels near Sabina Shoal
description: Sabina Shoal emerged as a new flashpoint when China surged up to 207 vessels into the area by September 10, the largest maritime militia deployment observed.

name: PLA Joint Sword-2024B Exercises Around Taiwan
target: Taiwan and outlying islands; key ports and blockade routes
description: China launched Joint Sword-2024B deploying 125 aircraft sorties, 17 PLAN ships, and 17 Coast Guard ships, featuring unprecedented Coast Guard blockade drills on Taiwan's major ports.

name: North Korea Tests Hwasong-19 ICBM
description: North Korea tested the Hwasong-19, a new solid-fueled ICBM that flew for 87 minutes to 7,000 km altitude — the longest ICBM flight in DPRK history, putting all of the contiguous US within range.

name: PLA First Combat Patrol of 2025 Around Taiwan
description: The PRC's first combat patrol of 2025 involved 22 military aircraft, with 18 crossing the Taiwan Strait median line.

name: PLA Strait Thunder-2025A Exercises Around Taiwan
target: Taiwan; simulated ports and energy infrastructure
description: The 33-hour exercise deployed 76 PLA aircraft, 15 PLAN ships, and the Shandong carrier strike group, with first-ever boarding inspection drills and live-fire strikes on simulated ports.

name: Carrier Shandong Sails Within 24nm of Taiwan
description: During Strait Thunder-2025A, the carrier Shandong maneuvered to within 24 nautical miles of Taiwan's coast — the closest a Chinese carrier had ever sailed to the island.

name: Carrier Shandong Approaches Within 600km of Guam
target: Western Pacific power projection near Guam
description: The Shandong strike group sailed east into the Pacific and came within 600 km of Guam before turning west, marking a significant extension of Chinese carrier operations.

name: Japan Launches Joint Operations Command
description: Japan launched its new Joint Operations Command, the most significant military command restructuring in decades, aimed at improving allied deterrence.

name: China Declares 'Nature Reserve' at Scarborough Shoal
description: China's State Council approved the 'Huangyan Dao National Nature Reserve' covering 3,524 hectares of Scarborough Shoal, within the Philippines' EEZ. Multiple nations rejected the move as 'lawfare.'

name: Carrier Fujian Achieves Initial Full-Deck Operational Capability
description: China released video showing complete electromagnetic catapult launch and recovery of J-15T, J-35 stealth fighters, and KJ-600 AWACS aircraft from the Fujian.

name: China Begins Major Island-Building at Antelope Reef
description: China began its first major artificial island-building since 2017, dredging approximately 1,490 acres at Antelope Reef — potentially surpassing Mischief Reef as the largest artificial island in the SCS.

name: Aircraft Carrier Fujian Commissioned into PLA Navy
description: The Fujian, China's first domestically designed carrier with electromagnetic catapults, was formally commissioned at Sanya with Xi Jinping presiding. China entered a three-carrier operational era.

name: Japan Announces Missile Deployment to Yonaguni Near Taiwan
target: Air defense coverage near Taiwan Strait
description: Japan confirmed plans to deploy Type-03 SAMs on Yonaguni Island, just 70 miles from Taiwan, as part of a 'missile archipelago' buildup across the Nansei chain backed by a record $58B defense budget.

name: China Deploys 352m Floating Barrier at Scarborough Shoal
description: China installed a 352-meter floating barrier to block Scarborough Shoal's lagoon entrance, accompanied by maritime militia vessels. The Philippine Coast Guard condemned it as illegal under international law.
